findings.title: — Hardcoded credentials, passwords, API keys, tokens, or TLS InsecureSkipVerify:
findings.description: The code contains hardcoded secrets (`apiKey` and `dbPassword`). This is a critical security violation.

findings.title: violation
findings.description: The use of `fmt.Println` for logging is not recommended. It lacks context and structure, making it difficult to filter and correlate logs.

findings.title: — `panic(...)` used for a recoverable error condition
findings.description: The function uses `panic` to handle an error that could be handled more gracefully, such as returning an error.

findings.title: Always wrap errors with context (HIGH) — Bare error returns without wrapping
findings.description: The error returned from `h.svc.GetAllUsers` is not wrapped using `fmt.Errorf`.

findings.title: Use TLS for All Network Communication (HIGH) — Insecure TLS configuration
findings.description: The HTTP client is created with `InsecureSkipVerify` set to true, which disables TLS verification. This is insecure and should never be used in production.

findings.title: Never Ignore Errors (HIGH)
findings.description: The error returned by `dblib.Insert` is ignored without context wrapping, which loses the call chain context. Use `fmt.Errorf with %w to wrap errors so the full trace is preserved for debugging.

findings.title: context.Context is not the first function parameter (MEDIUM) — Context should be
findings.description: The `context.Context` parameter is not the first argument in

findings.title: SEVERITY — Always wrap errors with context
findings.description: The error is returned without being wrapped with context, which loses the call chain context. Use `fmt.Errorf` with `%w` to wrap errors so the full trace is preserved for debugging.

findings.title: — Bare `return ..., err` without fmt.Errorf wrapping
findings.description: The function returns an error without wrapping it using `fmt.Errorf`. This loses the call chain context.
